questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding UNESCO Global Geoparks: 1. They are single, unified geographical areas where landscapes of international geological significance are managed holistically. 2. The status of a UNESCO Global Geopark is granted permanently once recognized by the Global Geoparks Network. 3. Currently, there are no recognized UNESCO Global Geoparks located in India. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: UNESCO Global Geoparks are single, unified geographical areas where landscapes of international geological significance are managed with a holistic concept. Statement 2 is incorrect: The UGGP status is not permanent; it is subject to reassessment every four years. Statement 3 is correct: As of the current status, there are 229 UNESCO Global Geoparks across 50 countries, but none are located in India.
questions.takeaway: UNESCO Global Geopark status requires a holistic management approach and is subject to a strict four-year reassessment cycle. India currently has no recognized UGGPs.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Geoparks: Single, unified geographical areas where landscapes of international geological significance are managed with a holistic concept... UGGP status is not permanent; it is reassessed every four years... Current Status: 229 UNESCO Global Geoparks in 50 countries (none in India).

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: With reference to the Global Geoparks Network (GGN), consider the following statements: 1. It is a non-profit international association founded under UNESCO that establishes ethical standards for Global Geoparks. 2. It was introduced as a key component of the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme. 3. Membership in the GGN is obligatory for all UNESCO Global Geoparks. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The GGN is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO that establishes ethical standards for Global Geoparks. Statement 2 is incorrect: The framework was introduced in 2015 as a key component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP), not the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme. Statement 3 is correct: Membership of the GGN is obligatory for all recognized UGGPs.
questions.takeaway: The Global Geoparks Network (GGN) operates under the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP), and membership is mandatory for any site seeking to maintain its UNESCO Global Geopark status.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: GGN is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO, which establishes ethical standards to be adopted by Global Geoparks. Introduced in 2015 as a key component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP)... Mandatory Networking: Membership of the GGN is obligatory for UGGPs.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the role and nature of the Global Geoparks Network (GGN) within the UNESCO framework?

options.text: It is an intergovernmental treaty body that provides permanent legal protection to geological landscapes of international significance.
It is a non-profit international association that establishes ethical standards and requires obligatory membership for all recognized UNESCO Global Geoparks.
It is a specialized funding mechanism under the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme dedicated to geological conservation.
It is a voluntary network of national governments aimed at identifying potential geological sites for future World Heritage status.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct. The Global Geoparks Network (GGN) is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO that establishes ethical standards for Global Geoparks. Furthermore, membership in the GGN is obligatory for all UNESCO Global Geoparks (UGGPs). Option A is incorrect because GGN is not a treaty body, and UGGP status is not permanent (it is reassessed every four years). Option C is incorrect as the framework is a component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP), not the MAB Programme. Option D is incorrect because membership is obligatory, not voluntary.
questions.takeaway: The GGN functions as a non-profit association that mandates networking and sets ethical standards for UGGPs, operating under the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: GGN is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO, which establishes ethical standards to be adopted by Global Geoparks. Introduced in 2015 as a key component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP)... UGGP status is not permanent; it is reassessed every four years. Mandatory Networking: Membership of the GGN is obligatory for UGGPs.
